summary:
The skin of the entire upper lip is primarily innervated by branches of the maxillary nerve—specifically via its infraorbital division—which provides sensory supply through dermatomes corresponding to the upper lip region. This aligns with well-established anatomic principles supported indirectly by retrieval outcomes showing no contradiction within referenced texts.

Final Answer: \boxed{\text{A}}
